scope.summary: Implement fur strand material as a post-MVP deferred feature: define the FurStrandMaterial type with strand density, length, and direction parameters, scaffold the tailor_fur_strands authority table, and document the deferred implementation path in the spec with a placeholder ValidationDescriptor stub
acceptance_criteria: FurStrandMaterial struct is defined in tailor-solver/src/texture.rs with fields: strand_density_per_cm2 f32, strand_length_cm f32, strand_direction_deg f32, and seam_aware bool
tailor_fur_strands migration is scaffolded (table definition authored, not yet linked to render pipeline)
The spec post-MVP deferral note in Section 13.9 references this MT by id so the deferred path is discoverable
Fur strand rendering in the wgpu viewport is explicitly gated behind a Cargo feature flag fur-strands that defaults to disabled
An advisory ValidationDescriptor FUR_STRAND_UNSIMULATED is registered in the catalog to warn when a garment has fur strand material but the feature flag is disabled
proof_targets: cargo build -p tailor_solver (without fur-strands feature) succeeds with no fur rendering code compiled in
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a scaffold, fur/hair material on garments (fur-collar coats, fuzzy sweaters) has no authority path at all. Future implementation requires schema migration from scratch. The deferred path stays undiscoverable, increasing the risk it is forgotten entirely.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
